markets.early_close_condition: This market will close and expire early if certified election results are published.
markets.no_sub_title: Rachida Dati, 5-10%
markets.rules_primary: If the margin of victory for Rachida Dati in the first round of the 2026 Paris mayoral election falls between 5% and 10%, inclusive of its lower bound and exclusive of its upper bound, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
The margin of victory shall be calculated as follows based on the margin type.

For percentage points: the vote percentage received by Rachida Dati minus the vote percentage received by the candidate/party/option that finishes immediately behind Rachida Dati if Rachida Dati wins, or the candidate/party that finishes first if Rachida Dati loses.
For raw votes: the total votes received by Rachida Dati minus the total votes received by the candidate/party/option that finishes immediately behind Rachida Dati if Rachida Dati wins, or the candidate/party/option that finishes first if Rachida Dati loses.
For electoral votes: the electoral votes received by Rachida Dati minus the electoral votes received by the candidate/party/option with the second-most electoral votes if Rachida Dati wins, or the electoral votes received by Rachida Dati minus the electoral votes received by the candidate/party/option with the most electoral votes if Rachida Dati does not.

Each margin range is inclusive of its lower bound and exclusive of its upper bound. No rounding shall be applied to the calculated margin. For example, for consecutive ranges of 5-6% and 6-7%, a margin of 5.99999% resolves within the 5-6% range, while a margin of exactly 6% resolves within the 6-7% range. If Rachida Dati runs under multiple parties or is listed multiple times, votes attributable to the same underlying natural Rachida Dati will be summed.

If Rachida Dati wins the election, the margin will be positive. If Rachida Dati loses the election, the margin will be negative. If Rachida Dati ties for first place, the margin shall be 0. For uncontested races where Rachida Dati is the only candidate, the margin shall be 100 percentage points or the total number of votes cast (for raw votes).
markets.title: Will the margin of victory for Rachida Dati in the the first round of the 2026 Paris mayoral election be between 5% and 10%?
markets.yes_sub_title: Rachida Dati, 5-10%

markets.no_sub_title: Sarah Knafo
markets.rules_primary: If Sarah Knafo is announced to qualify for the runoff in the 2026 Paris mayoral election, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary:
If a candidate wins the first round outright with a majority (or whatever threshold Paris requires to avoid a runoff), and no runoff occurs, the market for that winning candidate will resolve Yes and all other markets will resolve to No.

A candidate is "announced to qualify" when the official election authority of Paris certifies or announces qualification, OR when the Associated Press or at least two other Source Agencies report that Sarah Knafo has qualified for the runoff, whichever occurs first. Official certification is not required for resolution.

This is determined at the time qualification is announced; subsequent withdrawals, disqualifications, or changes in eligibility do not affect whether the candidate qualified. If a candidate is announced to not qualify, the market will immediately resolve to No.
markets.title: Will Sarah Knafo qualify for the runoff in the 2026 Paris mayoral election?
markets.yes_sub_title: Sarah Knafo
